NKJV.1: A Psalm of David when he was in the wilderness of Judah. O God, You are my God; Early will I seek You; My soul thirsts for You; My flesh longs for You In a dry and thirsty land Where there is no water.
NKJV.2: So I have looked for You in the sanctuary, To see Your power and Your glory.
NKJV.3: Because Your lovingkindness is better than life, My lips shall praise You.
NKJV.4: Thus I will bless You while I live; I will lift up my hands in Your name.
NKJV.5: My soul shall be satisfied as with marrow and fatness, And my mouth shall praise You with joyful lips.
NKJV.6: When I remember You on my bed, I meditate on You in the night watches.
NKJV.7: Because You have been my help, Therefore in the shadow of Your wings I will rejoice.
NKJV.8: My soul follows close behind You; Your right hand upholds me.
NKJV.9: But those who seek my life, to destroy it, Shall go into the lower parts of the earth.
NKJV.10: They shall fall by the sword; They shall be a portion for jackals.
NKJV.11: But the king shall rejoice in God;  Everyone who swears by Him shall glory; But the mouth of those who speak lies shall be stopped.

KJV.5: My soul<S>5315</S> shall be satisfied<S>7646</S> as with marrow<S>2459</S> and fatness<S>1880</S>; and my mouth<S>6310</S> shall praise<S>1984</S> thee with joyful<S>7445</S> lips<S>8193</S>: <sup>marrow: Heb. fatness</sup>
KJV.6: When I remember<S>2142</S> thee upon my bed<S>3326</S>, and meditate<S>1897</S> on thee in the night watches<S>821</S>.

ESV.1: O God, you are my God; earnestly I seek you; my soul thirsts for you; my flesh faints for you, as in a dry and weary land where there is no water.
ESV.2: So I have looked upon you in the sanctuary, beholding your power and glory.
ESV.3: Because your steadfast love is better than life, my lips will praise you.
ESV.4: So I will bless you as long as I live; in your name I will lift up my hands.
ESV.5: My soul will be satisfied as with fat and rich food, and my mouth will praise you with joyful lips,
ESV.6: when I remember you upon my bed, and meditate on you in the watches of the night;
ESV.7: for you have been my help, and in the shadow of your wings I will sing for joy.
ESV.8: My soul clings to you; your right hand upholds me.
ESV.9: But those who seek to destroy my life shall go down into the depths of the earth;
ESV.10: they shall be given over to the power of the sword; they shall be a portion for jackals.
ESV.11: But the king shall rejoice in God; all who swear by him shall exult, for the mouths of liars will be stopped.

NASB.1: A Psalm of David, when he was in the wilderness of Judah. O God, You are my God; I shall seek You earnestly; My soul thirsts for You, my flesh yearns for You, In a dry and weary land where there is no water.
NASB.2: Thus I have seen You in the sanctuary, To see Your power and Your glory.
NASB.3: Because Your lovingkindness is better than life, My lips will praise You.
NASB.4: So I will bless You as long as I live; I will lift up my hands in Your name.
NASB.5: My soul is satisfied as with marrow and fatness, And my mouth offers praises with joyful lips.
NASB.6: When I remember You on my bed, I meditate on You in the night watches,
NASB.7: For You have been my help, And in the shadow of Your wings I sing for joy.
NASB.8: My soul clings to You; Your right hand upholds me.
NASB.9: But those who seek my life to destroy it, Will go into the depths of the earth.
NASB.10: They will be delivered over to the power of the sword; They will be a prey for foxes.
NASB.11: But the king will rejoice in God; Everyone who swears by Him will glory, For the mouths of those who speak lies will be stopped.

NLT.1:
O God, you are my God;
I earnestly search for you.
My soul thirsts for you;
my whole body longs for you
in this parched and weary land
where there is no water.
NLT.2:
I have seen you in your sanctuary
and gazed upon your power and glory.
NLT.3:
Your unfailing love is better than life itself;
how I praise you!
NLT.4:
I will praise you as long as I live,
lifting up my hands to you in prayer.
NLT.5:
You satisfy me more than the richest feast.
I will praise you with songs of joy.
NLT.6:
I lie awake thinking of you,
meditating on you through the night.
NLT.7:
Because you are my helper,
I sing for joy in the shadow of your wings.
NLT.8:
I cling to you;
your strong right hand holds me securely.
NLT.9:
But those plotting to destroy me will come to ruin.
They will go down into the depths of the earth.
NLT.10:
They will die by the sword
and become the food of jackals.
NLT.11:
But the king will rejoice in God.
All who swear to tell the truth will praise him,
while liars will be silenced.

WEB.1: A Psalm by David, when he was in the desert of Judah. God, you are my God. I will earnestly seek you. My soul thirsts for you. My flesh longs for you, in a dry and weary land, where there is no water.
WEB.2: So I have seen you in the sanctuary, watching your power and your glory.
WEB.3: Because your loving kindness is better than life, my lips shall praise you.
WEB.4: So I will bless you while I live. I will lift up my hands in your name.
WEB.5: My soul shall be satisfied as with the richest food. My mouth shall praise you with joyful lips,
WEB.6: when I remember you on my bed, and think about you in the night watches.
WEB.7: For you have been my help. I will rejoice in the shadow of your wings.
WEB.8: My soul stays close to you. Your right hand holds me up.
WEB.9: But those who seek my soul, to destroy it, shall go into the lower parts of the earth.
WEB.10: They shall be given over to the power of the sword. They shall be jackal food.
WEB.11: But the king shall rejoice in God. Everyone who swears by him will praise him, for the mouth of those who speak lies shall be silenced.

CSB17.1:
A psalm of David. When he was in the Wilderness of Judah. ⓙ [1] God, you are my God; I eagerly seek you.
I thirst for you;
my body faints for you
in a land that is dry, desolate, and without water. ⓚ
CSB17.2:
So I gaze on you in the sanctuary
to see your strength and your glory. ⓛ
CSB17.3:
My lips will glorify you
because your faithful love is better than life. ⓜ
CSB17.4:
So I will bless you as long as I live;
at your name, I will lift up my hands. ⓝ
CSB17.5:
You satisfy me as with rich food; [1] ⓐ
my mouth will praise you with joyful lips. ⓑ
CSB17.6:
When I think of you as I lie on my bed,
I meditate on you during the night watches ⓒ
CSB17.7:
because you are my helper; ⓓ
I will rejoice in the shadow of your wings. ⓔ
CSB17.8:
I follow close to you;
your right hand holds on to me. ⓕ
CSB17.9:
But those who intend to destroy my life
will go into the depths of the earth. ⓖ
CSB17.10:
They will be given over to the power of the sword; ⓗ
they will become a meal for jackals.
CSB17.11:
But the king will rejoice in God; ⓘ
all who swear by him will boast,
for the mouths of liars will be shut. ⓙ

AMP.1:
A Psalm of David; when he was in the wilderness of Judah. O God, You are my God; with deepest longing I will seek You;
My soul [my life, my very self] thirsts for You, my flesh longs and sighs for You,
In a dry and weary land where there is no water.
AMP.2:
So I have gazed upon You in the sanctuary,
To see Your power and Your glory.
AMP.3:
Because Your lovingkindness is better than life,
My lips shall praise You.
AMP.4:
So will I bless You as long as I live;
I will lift up my hands in Your name.
AMP.5:
My soul [my life, my very self] is satisfied as with marrow and fatness,
And my mouth offers praises [to You] with joyful lips.
AMP.6:
When I remember You on my bed,
I meditate and thoughtfully focus on You in the night watches,
AMP.7:
For You have been my help,
And in the shadow of Your wings [where I am always protected] I sing for joy.
AMP.8:
My soul [my life, my very self] clings to You;
Your right hand upholds me.
AMP.9:
But those who seek my life to destroy it
Will [be destroyed and] go into the depths of the earth [into the underworld].
AMP.10:
They will be given over to the power of the sword;
They will be a prey for foxes.
AMP.11:
But the king will rejoice in God;
Everyone who swears by Him [honoring the true God, acknowledging His authority and majesty] will glory,
For the mouths of those who speak lies will be stopped.

MSG.1:
A David psalm, when he was out in the Judean wilderness.
God—you're my God!
I can't get enough of you!
I've worked up such hunger and thirst for God,
traveling across dry and weary deserts.
MSG.2:
So here I am in the place of worship, eyes open,
drinking in your strength and glory.
MSG.3:
In your generous love I am really living at last!
My lips brim praises like fountains.
MSG.4:
I bless you every time I take a breath;
My arms wave like banners of praise to you.
MSG.5:
I eat my fill of prime rib and gravy;
I smack my lips. It's time to shout praises!
MSG.6:
If I'm sleepless at midnight,
I spend the hours in grateful reflection.
MSG.7:
Because you've always stood up for me,
I'm free to run and play.
MSG.8:
I hold on to you for dear life,
and you hold me steady as a post.
MSG.9:
Those who are out to get me are marked for doom,
marked for death, bound for hell.
MSG.10:
They'll die violent deaths;
jackals will tear them limb from limb.
MSG.11:
But the king is glad in God;
his true friends spread the joy,
While small-minded gossips
are gagged for good.

NIV.1:
Psalm 63
A psalm of David. When he was in the Desert of Judah.
O God, you are my God,
earnestly I seek you;
my soul thirsts for you,
my body longs for you,
in a dry and weary land
where there is no water.
NIV.2:
I have seen you in the sanctuary
and beheld your power and your glory.
NIV.3:
Because your love is better than life,
my lips will glorify you.
NIV.4:
I will praise you as long as I live,
and in your name I will lift up my hands.
NIV.5:
My soul will be satisfied as with the richest of foods;
with singing lips my mouth will praise you.
NIV.6:
On my bed I remember you;
I think of you through the watches of the night.
NIV.7:
Because you are my help,
I sing in the shadow of your wings.
NIV.8:
My soul clings to you;
your right hand upholds me.
NIV.9:
They who seek my life will be destroyed;
they will go down to the depths of the earth.
NIV.10:
They will be given over to the sword
and become food for jackals.
NIV.11:
But the king will rejoice in God;
all who swear by God’s name will praise him,
while the mouths of liars will be silenced.

NRSVCE.1:
O God, you are my God, I seek you,
my soul thirsts for you;
my flesh faints for you,
as in a dry and weary land where there is no water.
NRSVCE.2:
So I have looked upon you in the sanctuary,
beholding your power and glory.
NRSVCE.3:
Because your steadfast love is better than life,
my lips will praise you.
NRSVCE.4:
So I will bless you as long as I live;
I will lift up my hands and call on your name.
NRSVCE.5:
My soul is satisfied as with a rich feast,
and my mouth praises you with joyful lips
NRSVCE.6:
when I think of you on my bed,
and meditate on you in the watches of the night;
NRSVCE.7:
for you have been my help,
and in the shadow of your wings I sing for joy.
NRSVCE.8:
My soul clings to you;
your right hand upholds me.
NRSVCE.9:
But those who seek to destroy my life
shall go down into the depths of the earth;
NRSVCE.10:
they shall be given over to the power of the sword,
they shall be prey for jackals.
NRSVCE.11:
But the king shall rejoice in God;
all who swear by him shall exult,
for the mouths of liars will be stopped.

NET.1: O God, you are my God! I long for you! My soul thirsts for you, my flesh yearns for you, in a dry and parched land where there is no water.
NET.2: Yes, in the sanctuary I have seen you, and witnessed your power and splendor.
NET.3: Because experiencing your loyal love is better than life itself, my lips will praise you.
NET.4: For this reason I will praise you while I live; in your name I will lift up my hands.
NET.5: As if with choice meat you satisfy my soul. My mouth joyfully praises you,
NET.6: whenever I remember you on my bed, and think about you during the nighttime hours.
NET.7: For you are my deliverer; under your wings I rejoice.
NET.8: My soul pursues you; your right hand upholds me.
NET.9: Enemies seek to destroy my life, but they will descend into the depths of the earth.
NET.10: Each one will be handed over to the sword; their corpses will be eaten by jackals.
NET.11: But the king will rejoice in God; everyone who takes oaths in his name will boast, for the mouths of those who speak lies will be shut up.
